title: JointOrigins Object
description: The collection of joint origins in this component. This provides access to all existing joint origins and supports the ability to create new joint origins.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createInput
methods.description: Creates a JointOriginInput object which is used to collect all of the information needed to create a simple joint origin. The creation of the input object takes the required input as the geometry argument and you can optionally use methods and properties on the created JointOriginInput to set other optional settings. The JointOrigin is created by calling the add method of the JointOrigins object and passing it the JointOriginInput object.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified joint origin using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Function that returns the specified joint origin using a name.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Returns number of joint origins in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Joint Origin Between Two Faces Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new Joint Origin between two planes.

related_samples.name: Joint Origin Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new Joint Origin.

add.title: JointOrigins.add Method
add.return_description: Returns a JointOrigin object if successfully created and null if it fails.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: A JointOriginInput object that full defines all of the information needed to create a joint origin. You create a JointOriginInput by using the createInput method of the JointOrigins object.

related_samples.name: Joint Origin Between Two Faces Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new Joint Origin between two planes.

related_samples.name: Joint Origin Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new Joint Origin.

classType.title: JointOrigins.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createInput.title: JointOrigins.createInput Method
createInput.description: Creates a JointOriginInput object which is used to collect all of the information needed to create a simple joint origin. The creation of the input object takes the required input as the geometry argument and you can optionally use methods and properties on the created JointOriginInput to set other optional settings. The JointOrigin is created by calling the add method of the JointOrigins object and passing it the JointOriginInput object.
createInput.return_description: Returns a JointOriginInput object if successfully created and null if it fails.

parameters.name: geometry
parameters.description: A JointGeometry object that defines the geometry the joint origin will be created on.

related_samples.name: Joint Origin Between Two Faces Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new Joint Origin between two planes.

related_samples.name: Joint Origin Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new Joint Origin.

item.title: JointOrigins.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified joint origin using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: JointOrigins.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Function that returns the specified joint origin using a name.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid name was specified.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the item within the collection to return.

count.title: JointOrigins.count Property
count.description: Returns number of joint origins in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = jointOrigins_var.count

isValid.title: JointOrigins.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:

propertyValue = jointOrigins_var.isValid

It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():
